sections.title: Who this pathway is for
sections.content: Adults with incidental or symptomatic ovarian cyst/mass findings in primary care; excludes known pregnancy-specific and paediatric pathways.

sections.title: Immediate same-day action
bullets: Acute severe unilateral pelvic pain +/- nausea/vomiting: suspect torsion/rupture -> emergency gynaecology.

sections.title: Urgent suspicion of cancer (USC) thresholds
bullets: Ascites or palpable pelvic/abdominal mass not clearly benign uterine/GI/urological origin.

sections.title: Premenopausal cyst thresholds
table: CA125 (+LDH/AFP/hCG if <40) and refer by risk

sections.title: Postmenopausal cyst thresholds
table: Repeat TVUS+CA125 at 6-month intervals up to 1 year

sections.title: RMI interpretation note
sections.content: Operational Scottish pathways commonly use RMI >200 for high-risk escalation; NICE specialist referral recommendation uses RMI >=250. If either threshold is reached locally, escalate via USC/specialist MDT.

sections.title: Safety net
bullets: If initial workup is non-diagnostic but symptoms persist/frequency increases, re-evaluate urgently.
CA125 can be normal in ovarian cancer; do not rely on CA125 alone.

sections.title: Evidence confidence
sections.content: Moderate-High (multisource concordance; minor RMI threshold variance and some extraction limitations).
